title: My pain is real. I deserve good care.
subtitle: An affirmation for advocating for yourself with medical systems
excerpt: For the moment before a medical appointment, after a dismissal, or when the doubt about whether the pain is real enough has started to take hold. A two-part statement for holding your ground.

practice.whenToUse: Before a medical appointment, after a dismissal, or when self-doubt about the validity of symptoms has arrived

content.text: Women are more likely to have pain dismissed by medical systems. Being told symptoms are anxiety or stress can cause self-doubt. That self-doubt makes things harder.
Use it before appointments, after dismissals, or when you start to agree with the dismissal.
Say this three times, out loud if you can:
My pain is real. I deserve good care.

Then write down three specific observations about your symptoms: specific, concrete, factual. Not "I feel terrible" but the particular details. Bring them to the appointment. Your specificity is your advocacy.
